transcript:
Agent: Thank you for calling AllCover Insurance. My name is Kevin. How can I help you today?

Customer: Hi Kevin. I filed a home insurance claim two weeks ago after a water leak damaged my kitchen floor and cabinets. I still have not heard anything and the contractor I hired is waiting for approval to start repairs.

Agent: I understand how frustrating that must be, Patricia. Let me pull up your claim right now. Can I have your policy number?

Customer: It is AC-2025-88341.

Agent: Thank you. I can see your claim was filed on April 28th for water damage. It is currently in the adjuster review stage. I can see notes here that an adjuster was assigned but there is no completed assessment yet.

Customer: It has been two weeks. I have a damaged kitchen and a contractor who cannot proceed. What is taking so long?

Agent: You are right to be concerned — our standard turnaround is 7 to 10 business days, and you are past that. I am escalating this claim to a senior adjuster right now and marking it as priority. You should expect a call from the adjuster within 24 hours.

Customer: I have heard that before. Is there anything you can make a note of so this actually happens?

Agent: Absolutely. I am adding a priority flag and a note with today's date and your contractor's waiting status. I am also sending you an email confirmation of this escalation right now so you have a reference number. The reference for this escalation is ESC-20250513-774.

Customer: What if I do not hear back in 24 hours?

Agent: If you have not been contacted by tomorrow at 5 PM, call us back and reference that escalation number — any agent will be able to see the priority flag and take immediate action. I am also making a personal note on my end.

Customer: Okay. What about the contractor? Can they start anything in the meantime?

Agent: I would advise waiting for adjuster approval to avoid any disputes over covered work. However, if there is any emergency mitigation needed to prevent further damage, that is typically covered and can proceed — please document everything with photos.

Customer: Alright. I will wait for the call tomorrow. Thank you for at least moving this forward.

Agent: I am sorry for the delay and I want to make sure this gets resolved for you quickly. Please do not hesitate to call back if you need anything.
